Multi-agent environment sync.

 {"id":"bd-11c9","title":"Add bubbletea ReleaseTerminal/RestoreTerminal lifecycle methods","description":"Add Bubbletea lifecycle methods ReleaseTerminal/RestoreTerminal to mirror Go API.

 {"id":"bd-11wv","title":"Bubbletea: track async event thread via TaskTracker","description":"# Bubbletea Async Event Thread Tracking

## Problem
In program.rs, the async event loop path (run_async / async_event_loop) spawns
an event polling thread at line ~1109:

    std::thread::spawn(move || {
        loop {
            if event_cancel.is_cancelled() { break; }
            match event::poll(Duration::from_millis(100)) { ... }
        }
    });

This thread uses a CancellationToken to know when to stop, but it is NOT
spawned via task_tracker.spawn_blocking(). The graceful shutdown sequence is:

1. cancel_token.cancel()          // signals all tracked tasks
2. task_tracker.close()           // prevents new tasks
3. task_tracker.wait() with 5s timeout  // waits for tracked tasks

Since the event thread is NOT tracked, step 3 doesn't wait for it. The thread
may still be blocked in event::poll() or event::read() when the async runtime
shuts down. This creates a race: the event thread may try to send on a dropped
tokio channel, causing a panic or silent failure.

## Why It Matters
- Graceful shutdown is incomplete — the event thread outlives the runtime
- In production SSH servers, accumulated leaked threads = resource exhaustion
- The 100ms poll timeout means the thread *usually* stops quickly, but "usually"
  is not "always" — event::read() can block indefinitely on some platforms

## Solution
Replace std::thread::spawn() with task_tracker.spawn_blocking() which:
- Registers the thread with TaskTracker
- Ensures graceful shutdown waits for it
- Proper cancellation via the existing CancellationToken

The spawn_blocking signature:
    task_tracker.spawn_blocking(move || { ... });

This is a minimal change — the thread body stays identical, only the spawn
mechanism changes.

## Key Files
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (async event loop, line ~1109)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Event thread spawned via task_tracker.spawn_blocking()
- Graceful shutdown waits for event thread
- CancellationToken still used for early exit
- Existing tests pass","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:47.300659380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:49:00.237212897Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11wv","depends_on_id":"bd-1s5p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:37:47.323443511Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":34,"issue_id":"bd-11wv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETION CRITERIA: This work stream is complete when subtask is done:
1. bd-2353: Spawn via task_tracker.spawn_blocking
Plus the testing bead bd-2i18 must pass.","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:32:26Z"},{"id":49,"issue_id":"bd-11wv","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"IMPLEMENTATION NOTE:
bd-2353 is a SINGLE LINE CHANGE:

Before: std::thread::spawn(move || { ... });
After:  task_tracker.spawn_blocking(move || { ... });

The task_tracker variable is already in scope at line 1110.

CRITICAL PATH: bd-2353 → bd-2i18 → bd-1azg","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:49:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-11zs","title":"Property Tests: lipgloss dimension calculations","description":"Property-based tests for lipgloss width/height calculations.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify dimension calculation invariants
across all style combinations.

## Properties to Test

### Width Invariants
- [ ] output_width >= content_width (when width set)
- [ ] output_width == width when width > content_width
- [ ] padding adds exactly padding_left + padding_right
- [ ] margin adds exactly margin_left + margin_right

### Height Invariants
- [ ] output_height >= content_lines
- [ ] padding adds exactly padding_top + padding_bottom
- [ ] border adds exactly 2 (top + bottom) when present

### Unicode Width
- [ ] Wide chars (CJK) count as 2
- [ ] Combining chars count as 0
- [ ] Emoji width handled correctly
- [ ] ANSI sequences don't affect width

### Composition
- [ ] inherit() preserves dimension calculations
- [ ] copy() produces identical dimensions
- [ ] nested styles compute correctly

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn width_never_less_than_content(
        content in "[\\w\\s]{0,100}",
        width in 0usize..200,
        padding in 0usize..20,
    ) {
        let style = Style::new()
            .width(width)
            .padding_horizontal(padding);
        let output = style.render(&content);
        assert!(visual_width(&output) >= visual_width(&content));
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- Dimensions always calculated correctly
- Unicode edge cases handled
- No panics for any style combination","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:06.723939917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:26:51.659766118Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:26:51.659630014Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["lipgloss","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-11zs","depends_on_id":"bd-3m5y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:57:57.783744084Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-11zs","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:06.743725346Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1327","title":"Bubbletea: unit tests for sync thread lifecycle","description":"# Unit Tests for Sync Thread Lifecycle

## Purpose
Verify that the sync event loop properly captures JoinHandles and joins threads
on shutdown. These tests ensure the thread lifecycle fixes actually work.

## Test Cases

### 1. test_thread_handles_captured
Verify that all three spawned threads (external forwarder, input parser, command
executor) return JoinHandles that can be stored.

### 2. test_threads_exit_on_channel_drop
Verify that threads exit when their input channels are dropped.

### 3. test_shutdown_joins_all_threads
Integration test using a mock Program that spawns threads and verifies all
join within timeout.

### 4. test_join_timeout_logs_warning
Verify that if a thread doesnt join within timeout, a warning is logged.

## Logging Requirements
All tests should use tracing_test::traced_test to capture log output and
verify expected log messages appear.

## Key File
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs (add tests to existing test module)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All 4 test cases implemented and passing
- Tests use tracing_test for log verification
- Tests are deterministic (no flaky timing issues)
- Tests document expected behavior in comments","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:28:34.479166183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T23:46:48.155085615Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1327","depends_on_id":"bd-1dsp","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:28:34.495744040Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1327","depends_on_id":"bd-3azk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:29:44.784071319Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":38,"issue_id":"bd-1327","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"NOTE: Test case 4 (test_join_timeout_logs_warning) should be renamed to test_slow_thread_join_logs_debug since we dont implement explicit timeout. The test verifies:
1. Thread joins eventually complete
2. Debug logging shows join progress
3. If a thread panics, warn logging shows the panic

Rename test case 4 to: test_thread_panic_logs_warning
Verify: match handle.join() logs Err case with tracing::warn","created_at":"2026-01-29T23:46:48Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-13np","title":"demo_showcase core: bootstrap App from Config (seed/theme/toggles)","description":"Implement the single, canonical bootstrap path from `demo_showcase::Config` to a fully initialized `App` model.

Why:
- Prevents scattered initialization logic.
- Makes headless testing and `--self-check` deterministic.

Deliverables:
- `App::new(config: Config) -> App` (or similar) that:
  - initializes theme context/preset
  - initializes domain data from seed
  - initializes page/router state
  - applies toggles (mouse/animations/no-color/no-alt-screen)

Acceptance:
- All entrypoints (interactive, self-check, ssh mode) use this same bootstrap.
- Bootstrapping is unit-testable.","notes":"Implemented App::from_config canonical bootstrap path with seed support. All 8 new tests pass.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:06.629120905Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T05:05:53.437092906Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T05:05:53.436968945Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bootstrap","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-2q95","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:20.218711349Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-3bz7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:21.491726389Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:06.642870498Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-3mh2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:23.046165231Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-3s4n","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:24.848608444Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-13np","depends_on_id":"bd-ab35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:37:18.934928904Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-14v4","title":"demo_showcase core: navigation sidebar (List + filtering)","description":"Build a polished navigation sidebar that makes the multi-page app feel cohesive.

Why:
- Navigation is constant UI; if it feels clunky, the whole app feels clunky.
- It’s also a great place to showcase bubbles list + filtering.

Requirements:
- Nav list:
  - use `bubbles::list` (filterable) or a custom list if needed
  - entries for Dashboard/Jobs/Docs/Wizard/Files/Logs/Settings
  - clear selected/current page highlight
- Filtering:
  - quick filter input that narrows nav items
  - escape clears filter and restores full list
- Mouse (optional):
  - click to navigate

Integration:
- Navigation emits a single clean app-level route message (no page-to-page coupling).

Acceptance:
- Switching pages feels instant.
- Filtering nav demonstrates textinput+list integration and feels fast.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:58.826538621Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:51.210183012Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T03:48:51.210116268Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbles","demo_showcase"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-14v4","depends_on_id":"bd-28jp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:19.445789703Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-14v4","depends_on_id":"bd-2k35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:17:18.267533574Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-14v4","depends_on_id":"bd-3j9x","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:07:58.851229842Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-15ay","title":"Add textarea word transformation operations (Capitalize, Uppercase, Lowercase, Transpose)","description":"Add textarea word transformation operations (Capitalize, Uppercase, Lowercase, Transpose) to match Go Huh behavior.

Scope:
- Implement word operations in textarea field logic (likely part of bd-212m.4.1 / bd-1o6y).
- Ensure operations respect cursor position and Unicode word boundaries.
- Add unit tests for each operation with edge cases.","acceptance_criteria":"Criteria:
- Word transform operations behave identically to Go (cursor position + selection).
- Unit tests cover ASCII and Unicode word boundaries.
- Conformance fixtures updated if Go has explicit tests for these ops.","notes":"Files: crates/huh/src/lib.rs (textarea editing helpers). Consider using unicode-segmentation for word boundaries.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-25T01:09:46.899949324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-25T18:10:24.270791587Z","closed_at":"2026-01-25T18:10:24.270718109Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-228s","title":"Unit Tests: harmonica animations","description":"Unit tests for harmonica spring physics animations.

## Current State
harmonica has NO tests directory - critical gap!

## Test Requirements

### Spring Animation
- [ ] spring creation with various stiffness/damping
- [ ] update() frame calculations
- [ ] value convergence to target
- [ ] velocity decay behavior
- [ ] is_done() completion detection

### FPS Control
- [ ] limiter initialization
- [ ] frame timing accuracy
- [ ] delta time calculations

### Edge Cases
- [ ] zero stiffness (no spring)
- [ ] zero damping (infinite oscillation)
- [ ] very high stiffness (snap)
- [ ] negative targets
- [ ] very small movements

### Numerical Stability
- [ ] large time deltas
- [ ] very small values
- [ ] floating point edge cases
- [ ] accumulated error bounds

## Test Approach
Since harmonica is purely mathematical:
- Use deterministic test inputs
- Verify against known physics formulas
- Test convergence within tolerance
- Property tests ideal for this crate

## Success Criteria
- All public API has tests
- Physics calculations verified against reference
- Numerical edge cases handled gracefully","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:52:18.253558955Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T20:37:47.911645643Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T20:37:47.911516473Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["harmonica","testing","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-228s","depends_on_id":"bd-28co","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:52:18.282929615Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22c0","title":"Files: markdown preview via glamour","description":"If the selected file is Markdown, render it with glamour for a *showcase-grade* preview.

Why:
- This demonstrates that glamour is not just for a dedicated Docs page; it can be embedded anywhere.
- It makes the Files page feel like a real tool (browse a repo, preview README/config docs, etc.).

Requirements:
- Detect markdown by extension and/or simple content heuristics.
- Render via glamour using:
  - current theme (match the app)
  - current width (re-render on resize)
  - syntax highlighting / line numbers toggles if enabled by Config/Settings
- Display the rendered output inside the preview viewport.
- Provide a fallback mode for no-color/ASCII (still readable, no ANSI).

Acceptance:
- Markdown preview looks great, scrolls smoothly, and updates immediately when theme/toggles change.
- This path is covered by the Files E2E scenario (selecting a markdown fixture visibly renders).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:20.507671596Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T18:20:04.652990666Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T18:20:04.652863269Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","files","glamour"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22c0","depends_on_id":"bd-1wtl","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:17.604172078Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-22c0","depends_on_id":"bd-201t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:11:20.526217700Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-22c0","depends_on_id":"bd-7jxu","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:20:20.229876332Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-22vc","title":"demo_showcase page: Docs (glamour markdown viewer)","description":"In-app documentation / knowledge base rendered by `glamour`.

Why:
- A premium tool is self-documenting: users shouldn’t need to leave the app to learn keybindings or understand what they’re seeing.
- It’s also the best place to prove `glamour` is genuinely production-quality (wrapping, themes, code blocks, long docs).

Must showcase:
- `glamour` rendering quality:
  - width-aware wrapping and layout
  - headings/lists/tables, emphasis, blockquotes
  - code blocks with good styling (and optional syntax highlighting)
  - link formatting that’s readable in a terminal
- Themes and toggles:
  - respects the app theme and `--no-color`/ASCII modes
  - optional “line numbers” toggle for code blocks
  - optional syntax highlighting (feature-gated) with a safe fallback
- Viewer ergonomics:
  - viewport scrolling with smooth navigation (page/half-page, top/bottom)
  - in-doc search with match navigation (next/prev)
  - docs navigation sidebar/list (multiple docs pages + preview)

Content requirements:
- Include at least:
  - “Welcome / What is this app?” (product story)
  - “Keybindings / Commands”
  - “Architecture overview” (bubbletea update/view/cmd model)
  - “Pages” entries (one per page describing what it demonstrates)
- Prefer authoring purpose-built markdown for the demo (not just dumping README).

Acceptance:
- Markdown looks genuinely great and readable.
- Search is fast, stable, and works with long documents.

Testing:
- Unit/snapshot tests:
  - render stability for fixed width/height
  - search match navigation correctness
  - theme/no-color toggles produce expected output
- E2E:
  - docs render + search + resize reflow scenario (captures artifacts + JSONL logs).

Notes:
- Do not let this page become a “toy markdown viewer”; treat it like a serious docs UI.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:18.953726765Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:17:13.973264572Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:17:13.973123550Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo_showcase","docs","glamour","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-22vc","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:18.974478560Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-2353","title":"Bubbletea async: spawn event thread via task_tracker.spawn_blocking","description":"# Replace std::thread::spawn with task_tracker.spawn_blocking for event thread

## What
In program.rs async_event_loop(), line ~1109, change:

    std::thread::spawn(move || {
        loop {
            if event_cancel.is_cancelled() { break; }
            match event::poll(Duration::from_millis(100)) { ... }
        }
    });

To:

    task_tracker.spawn_blocking(move || {
        loop {
            if event_cancel.is_cancelled() { break; }
            match event::poll(Duration::from_millis(100)) { ... }
        }
    });

This registers the thread with the TaskTracker so that the graceful shutdown
path (task_tracker.close() + task_tracker.wait()) properly waits for it.

The task_tracker variable is already in scope — it's used for the command
execution task and the external message task in the same function.

## Considerations
- spawn_blocking() returns a JoinHandle<T> from tokio. We don't need the
  return value, so just drop it (same as current behavior).
- The CancellationToken (event_cancel) still handles early exit. The
  TaskTracker just ensures the shutdown path waits for the thread.
- The 100ms poll timeout means the thread checks cancellation every 100ms.
  This is fast enough for graceful shutdown.

## Key File
- crates/bubbletea/src/program.rs, fn async_event_loop() line ~1109","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:39:21.838919726Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-30T01:14:51.568178599Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bubbletea","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2353","depends_on_id":"bd-11wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-29T17:39:21.863420425Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-244a","title":"glamour: Add LRU cache limit to StyleCache","description":"# Task: Add Size Limit to StyleCache

## Problem Statement
The StyleCache in syntax.rs uses unbounded Vec storage:

\`\`\`rust
pub struct StyleCache {
    cache: Vec<(SynStyle, LipglossStyle)>,  // No size limit!
}

impl StyleCache {
    pub fn get_or_convert(&mut self, syn_style: &SynStyle) -> &LipglossStyle {
        // Linear search O(n)
        if let Some(idx) = self.cache.iter().position(|(s, _)| s == syn_style) {
            return &self.cache[idx].1;
        }
        // Add new entry - unbounded growth
        self.cache.push((syn_style.clone(), lip_style));
        &self.cache.last().unwrap().1
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Impact Analysis
- **Memory**: Large code files with many unique styles accumulate entries
- **Performance**: Linear search degrades as cache grows
- **No cleanup**: Entries never removed, even for completed documents

## Solution Design

### Use LRU Cache with Fixed Size
\`\`\`rust
use lru::LruCache;
use std::num::NonZeroUsize;

pub struct StyleCache {
    cache: LruCache<SynStyle, LipglossStyle>,
}

impl StyleCache {
    const DEFAULT_CAPACITY: usize = 256;
    
    pub fn new() -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LruCache::new(NonZeroUsize::new(Self::DEFAULT_CAPACITY).unwrap()),
        }
    }
    
    pub fn with_capacity(capacity: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            cache: LruCache::new(NonZeroUsize::new(capacity.max(1)).unwrap()),
        }
    }
    
    pub fn get_or_convert(&mut self, syn_style: &SynStyle) -> LipglossStyle {
        if let Some(style) = self.cache.get(syn_style) {
            return style.clone();
        }
        let lip_style = convert_style(syn_style);
        self.cache.put(syn_style.clone(), lip_style.clone());
        lip_style
    }
    
    /// Returns the current number of cached entries.
    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {
        self.cache.len()
    }
    
    /// Returns true if the cache is empty.
    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
        self.cache.is_empty()
    }
    
    /// Clears all cached entries.
    pub fn clear(&mut self) {
        self.cache.clear();
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Why LRU?
- Simple API, well-tested crate
- O(1) get and put operations
- Automatic eviction of least-recently-used entries
- Common styles (comments, keywords) stay cached

## Implementation Steps
1. Add \`lru\` crate as dependency
2. Replace Vec with LruCache
3. Update get_or_convert to use LRU API (returns clone, not reference)
4. Add configurable capacity
5. Add len(), is_empty(), clear() methods
6. Add benchmarks comparing old vs new
7. Test cache eviction behavior

## Testing Strategy

### Unit Tests
\`\`\`rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod style_cache_tests {
    use super::*;

    fn make_style(id: u8) -> SynStyle {
        SynStyle {
            foreground: syntect::highlighting::Color { r: id, g: 0, b: 0, a: 255 },
            background: syntect::highlighting::Color::WHITE,
            font_style: syntect::highlighting::FontStyle::empty(),
        }
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_default_capacity() {
        let cache = StyleCache::new();
        assert_eq!(cache.capacity(), 256);
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_custom_capacity() {
        let cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(100);
        // Note: actual capacity might be slightly different due to LRU internals
        assert!(cache.capacity() >= 100);
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_respects_limit() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(3);
        
        // Add 4 different styles
        for i in 0..4 {
            let style = make_style(i);
            cache.get_or_convert(&style);
        }
        
        // Cache should have exactly 3 entries (oldest evicted)
        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 3);
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_hit_returns_same_style() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(10);
        let style = make_style(42);
        
        let first = cache.get_or_convert(&style);
        let second = cache.get_or_convert(&style);
        
        // Should return identical styles
        assert_eq!(first, second);
    }

    #[test]
    fn lru_eviction_behavior() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(2);
        
        let s1 = make_style(1);
        let s2 = make_style(2);
        let s3 = make_style(3);
        
        cache.get_or_convert(&s1);  // [s1]
        cache.get_or_convert(&s2);  // [s1, s2]
        cache.get_or_convert(&s1);  // [s2, s1] - s1 moved to back (recently used)
        cache.get_or_convert(&s3);  // [s1, s3] - s2 evicted (LRU)
        
        // s1 should still be cached (recently used)
        // s2 should be evicted (least recently used)
        // s3 should be cached (just added)
        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 2);
        
        // Verify by checking if lookup requires conversion
        // (This is an indirect test - ideally track cache misses)
    }

    #[test]
    fn cache_clear() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(10);
        cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(1));
        cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(2));
        
        assert!(!cache.is_empty());
        
        cache.clear();
        
        assert!(cache.is_empty());
        assert_eq!(cache.len(), 0);
    }

    #[test]
    fn many_unique_styles_bounded() {
        let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(100);
        
        // Add 1000 unique styles
        for i in 0u8..=255 {
            for j in 0u8..4 {
                let style = SynStyle {
                    foreground: syntect::highlighting::Color { r: i, g: j, b: 0, a: 255 },
                    background: syntect::highlighting::Color::WHITE,
                    font_style: syntect::highlighting::FontStyle::empty(),
                };
                cache.get_or_convert(&style);
            }
        }
        
        // Cache should be bounded at capacity
        assert!(cache.len() <= 100);
    }
}
\`\`\`

### Benchmark Tests
\`\`\`rust
use criterion::{criterion_group, criterion_main, Criterion, BenchmarkId};

fn bench_cache_hits(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(256);
    let style = make_style(42);
    cache.get_or_convert(&style);  // Prime cache
    
    c.bench_function("cache_hit", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            cache.get_or_convert(&style)
        });
    });
}

fn bench_cache_misses(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(256);
    let mut counter = 0u8;
    
    c.bench_function("cache_miss", |b| {
        b.iter(|| {
            counter = counter.wrapping_add(1);
            cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(counter))
        });
    });
}

fn bench_cache_under_pressure(c: &mut Criterion) {
    let mut group = c.benchmark_group("cache_pressure");
    
    for capacity in [16, 64, 256, 1024] {
        group.bench_with_input(
            BenchmarkId::new("lookup", capacity),
            &capacity,
            |b, &cap| {
                let mut cache = StyleCache::with_capacity(cap);
                let mut counter = 0u8;
                
                b.iter(|| {
                    counter = counter.wrapping_add(1);
                    cache.get_or_convert(&make_style(counter))
                });
            },
        );
    }
    group.finish();
}

criterion_group!(benches, bench_cache_hits, bench_cache_misses, bench_cache_under_pressure);
criterion_main!(benches);
\`\`\`

### E2E Test Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# Test: glamour_style_cache_e2e.sh

set -e
echo "=== glamour StyleCache LRU Tests ==="

# Run unit tests
echo "Running unit tests..."
cargo test --package glamour style_cache -- --nocapture 2>&1 | tee /tmp/style_cache.log

# Run benchmarks
echo ""
echo "Running benchmarks..."
cargo bench --package glamour -- cache 2>&1 | tee /tmp/style_cache_bench.log

# Memory check (if valgrind available)
if command -v valgrind &> /dev/null; then
    echo ""
    echo "Running memory check..."
    valgrind --leak-check=full cargo test --package glamour style_cache_memory 2>&1 | tee /tmp/style_cache_mem.log
    
    if grep -q "definitely lost: 0 bytes" /tmp/style_cache_mem.log; then
        echo "[PASS] No memory leaks detected"
    else
        echo "[WARN] Potential memory issues - review /tmp/style_cache_mem.log"
    fi
fi

echo ""
echo "=== Results ==="
if grep -q "FAILED" /tmp/style_cache.log; then
    echo "[FAIL] Some tests failed"
    exit 1
else
    echo "[PASS] All StyleCache tests passed"
fi
\`\`\`

## Performance Notes
- LRU cache uses HashMap internally: O(1) operations
- Small overhead for maintaining LRU order
- Memory usage now bounded: capacity * sizeof(style) bytes max
- Default capacity 256 should cover most syntax themes

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] StyleCache uses LRU with configurable capacity
- [ ] Default capacity of 256 entries
- [ ] O(1) lookup performance maintained
- [ ] Memory usage bounded
- [ ] Unit tests verify eviction behavior
- [ ] Benchmarks compare performance
- [ ] E2E test script validates functionality","notes":"LavenderMarsh claiming - implementing LRU cache for StyleCache","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:50.377147329Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T17:12:11.009484860Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T17:12:11.009406734Z","close_reason":"done","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","memory","performance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-244a","depends_on_id":"bd-8wlh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:20:50.397094415Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-awkz","title":"lipgloss: Fix incomplete OSC sequence termination handling","description":"# Bug: Incomplete OSC Sequence Termination Handling

## Problem Statement
In lib.rs lines 403-411, the OSC sequence handling doesn't properly validate
the String Terminator (ST) format:

```rust
State::Osc => {
    if c == '\x07' {
        state = State::Normal;  // BEL terminator - correct
    } else if c == '\x1b' {
        // Handle ST (ESC \) - we see ESC, transition to Esc to handle the backslash
        state = State::Esc;  // BUG: doesn't verify \ follows
    }
}
```

## Technical Background
OSC (Operating System Command) sequences have format:
```
ESC ] Ps ; Pt BEL      (7-bit, BEL terminator)
ESC ] Ps ; Pt ESC \    (7-bit, ST terminator)
```

The current code sees ESC and transitions to Esc state, but:
1. Doesn't verify the next character is \
2. If the next char is [, it would incorrectly start a new CSI sequence
3. Incomplete ST at end of string leaves state in Esc

## Example Malformed Input
```
\x1b]0;title\x1b[31m  (OSC followed immediately by CSI - no proper terminator)
```
Current behavior: After seeing \x1b in OSC, transitions to Esc, then [ transitions to Csi.
The "0;title" is ignored, and "[31m" is treated as a CSI sequence.

This is actually somewhat correct behavior (recovering from malformed input),
but we should explicitly handle this case rather than relying on state machine quirks.

## Solution Design

### Option A: Strict ST Validation
Add a dedicated state for "seen ESC in OSC, expecting \":
```rust
enum State { Normal, Esc, Csi, Osc, OscEsc }

State::Osc => {
    if c == '\x07' {
        state = State::Normal;
    } else if c == '\x1b' {
        state = State::OscEsc;
    }
}

State::OscEsc => {
    if c == '\\' {
        state = State::Normal;  // Valid ST
    } else if c == '[' {
        state = State::Csi;     // Malformed OSC, start of new CSI
    } else {
        state = State::Esc;     // Other escape sequence
    }
}
```

### Option B: Lenient Recovery (RECOMMENDED)
Keep current behavior but document it explicitly:
```rust
State::Osc => {
    // OSC sequence: ESC ] Ps ; Pt (BEL | ESC \)
    if c == '\x07' {
        // BEL terminator
        state = State::Normal;
    } else if c == '\x1b' {
        // Start of ST (ESC \) OR malformed input followed by new escape.
        // Transition to Esc state which will handle either case correctly:
        // - If next char is \, Esc state ignores it (simple escape)
        // - If next char is [, Esc state transitions to Csi (new sequence)
        state = State::Esc;
    }
    // All other characters are part of the OSC payload, ignored for width
}
```

## Testing Strategy
```rust
#[test]
fn visible_width_osc_termination() {
    // BEL terminator
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title\x07text"), 4);
    
    // ST terminator (ESC \)
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title\x1b\\text"), 4);
    
    // Malformed: OSC followed by CSI (no terminator)
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title\x1b[31mred\x1b[0m"), 3);
    
    // Truncated OSC at end
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]0;title"), 0);
    
    // Empty OSC
    assert_eq!(visible_width("\x1b]\x07text"), 4);
}
```

## Dependency
This task depends on bd-10h6 (consolidation) because we only need to fix
this in one place after consolidation.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OSC termination properly handles both BEL and ST
- [ ] Malformed input recovers gracefully
- [ ] Tests cover all termination scenarios
- [ ] Behavior documented in code comments","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:17:42.853402879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:20:17.318529861Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:20:17.318460262Z","close_reason":"Added OscEsc state to visible_width() for explicit ST validation. Handles ESC+backslash (valid ST), ESC+[ (malformed->CSI recovery), ESC+] (malformed->new OSC), and other malformed cases. Added 13 new tests covering hyperlinks, ST termination, truncated OSC, and malformed sequences. All 226 lipgloss tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ansi-parsing","correctness","lipgloss"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-awkz","depends_on_id":"bd-10h6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:17:42.873853491Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-awkz","depends_on_id":"bd-2nw3","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T02:17:42.869057698Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

-{"id":"bd-cnqe","title":"Glamour: evaluate and add LRU crate dependency","description":"# Evaluate and add LRU dependency to glamour

## What
Decide which LRU implementation to use and add it to glamour's Cargo.toml.

Options:
1. lru crate (https://crates.io/crates/lru) — most popular, 80M+ downloads,
   MIT licensed, provides LruCache<K,V> with O(1) get/put/pop.
2. indexmap crate — already in many Cargo ecosystems, provides O(1) get +
   swap_remove. Not a true LRU but approximates one.
3. Hand-rolled with HashMap + VecDeque — no new dependency but more code.

Check if lru or indexmap is already a transitive dependency in the workspace
(cargo tree -p glamour | grep -i lru). Prefer reusing an existing dependency.

## Key File

 {"id":"bd-dj49","title":"Property Tests: glamour text wrapping","description":"Property-based tests for glamour text wrapping algorithms.

## Purpose
Use proptest to verify text wrapping invariants
for all possible inputs.

## Properties to Test

### Line Width
- [ ] No line exceeds max_width (visual)
- [ ] Words not broken unless necessary
- [ ] Hyphenation only when word > width

### Content Preservation
- [ ] All words present in output
- [ ] Word order preserved
- [ ] No characters lost or added

### ANSI Preservation
- [ ] ANSI codes don't count toward width
- [ ] ANSI codes preserved across wrap
- [ ] Style reset at end of each line

### Unicode Handling
- [ ] Wide chars (CJK) count correctly
- [ ] Combining marks stay with base
- [ ] Emoji not split across lines

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty string wraps to empty
- [ ] Single very long word
- [ ] Only whitespace
- [ ] Only ANSI codes

## Test Strategy

```rust
proptest! {
    #[test]
    fn wrap_preserves_all_words(
        text in "[\\w\\s]{0,500}",
        width in 10usize..100,
    ) {
        let wrapped = wrap(&text, width);
        let original_words: HashSet<_> = text.split_whitespace().collect();
        let wrapped_words: HashSet<_> = wrapped.split_whitespace().collect();
        assert_eq!(original_words, wrapped_words);
    }
}
```

## Success Criteria
- Wrapping never loses content
- Width limits always respected
- Unicode edge cases handled","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:35.890366940Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-28T21:45:27.694755297Z","closed_at":"2026-01-28T21:45:27.694614655Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["glamour","property","proptest","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dj49","depends_on_id":"bd-15c6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:58:02.318354105Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-dj49","depends_on_id":"bd-ndn1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-28T18:56:35.916653701Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"bd-dkff","title":"demo_showcase page: Dashboard (metrics, status, animations)","description":"The flagship “wow” page: a premium monitoring-style dashboard that immediately communicates polish, usefulness, and technical depth.

Why:
- First impressions matter: on first launch, the dashboard should make the project look inevitable.
- A dashboard is the best place to integrate *everything at once*: layout, styling, animation, real-time updates, and drill-down.

What this page must showcase (integrated):
- lipgloss: dense but readable card/grid layout, borders, alignment, width-aware wrapping/truncation, theme slots.
- bubbles: progress/spinner widgets embedded in cards, a compact table/list for “top incidents / hot services”, viewport for detail text.
- harmonica: smooth animated counters/progress (deltas, easing), attention cues (subtle pulse on state changes).
- bubbletea: timed updates (tick), resize responsiveness, optional mouse interactions.
- charmed_log/toasts: state transitions emit a toast/alert and optionally log an event.

Core UX requirements:
- Multiple “cards” with distinct semantics (e.g., Requests, Latency, Error Rate, Job Throughput, Incidents).
- Visible health states (ok/warn/error) with consistent color + iconography.
- Drill-down: selecting/clicking a card opens a modal/panel with detail + explanation.
- Responsive: reflow to 1-column / 2-column / 3-column depending on terminal width.

Data requirements:
- Driven by the deterministic simulation engine (seeded) so demos/tests are reproducible.
- Includes plausible trends (up/down arrows), thresholds, and at least one periodic “incident”.

Acceptance:
- Looks and feels like a production ops dashboard.
- Values visibly change over time without flicker or input lag.
- State transitions are obvious (animated + toast), but never noisy.

Testing:
- Unit tests:
  - health-state derivation (thresholds + hysteresis if used)
  - trend calculation (delta over N ticks)
  - responsive layout breakpoints (given width/height)
- E2E:
  - covered by smoke tour
  - additional assertions in the settings/theme scenario should confirm dashboard updates immediately after theme/toggle changes.

Notes:
- Prefer “information clarity” motion (animate toward truth) over gratuitous animation.
- Keep all motion disable-able via `--no-animations` / reduce motion.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:15.448098103Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T01:33:30.045415432Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T01:33:30.045346414Z","close_reason":"Verified complete: dashboard.rs (1,846 LOC) implements premium ops dashboard with: multiple stat cards, services/deployments/jobs sections, health states (Healthy/Degraded/Unhealthy), Animator integration for smooth transitions, responsive width-based layout, drill-down via DetailsPanel, simulation-driven deterministic data. Tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dashboard","demo_showcase","page"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-dkff","depends_on_id":"bd-2pzh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-27T22:09:15.462915847Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
